description: COALESCE with multiple nullable args but a literal as the last argument is non-nullable

columns.name: result
columns.note: last argument is a literal, guaranteeing non-NULL

name: coalesce_with_literal_last
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserDisplayText
-- @returns :one
SELECT COALESCE(bio, age::text, 'unknown') AS result FROM users WHERE id = $1
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe checks all COALESCE arguments and determines non-nullability when any argument is a non-null literal or NOT NULL column
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks the result as nullable because the first argument is nullable
